question.text: An individual investment adviser must transition to a non-individual investment adviser registration when:

answer.text: The number of clients exceeds 500 or fees exceed Rs.5 crore, whichever is earlier
The number of clients exceeds 300 or fees exceed Rs.3 crore in a financial year, whichever is earlier
The number of clients exceeds 200 or fees exceed Rs.2 crore, whichever is earlier
The number of clients exceeds 1,000 at any point in time

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Under SEBI IA Regulations, an individual IA whose clients exceed 300 at any point or whose fees exceed Rs.3 crore in a financial year (whichever is earlier) must apply for in-principle registration as a non-individual IA. **The Trap:** The Rs.5 crore and 500 client figures are fabricated distractors. The actual thresholds are 300 clients and Rs.3 crore. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series X-A covers the same registration thresholds. This is a key renewal exam topic as these limits were introduced in the 2020 amendments. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Track both metrics simultaneously — even one crossing the threshold triggers the transition requirement. The in-principle approval is valid for 3 months.
